title: Grabette opens a lower-cost route to robot training data
friendlyTitle: An open handheld tool collects training examples for robots
summary: Grabette is an open handheld gripper that records human manipulation demonstrations and converts them into robot-ready datasets. The project publishes its hardware, capture software and processing pipeline, with an estimated bill of materials of about €490 for the recorder and €120 for its robotic gripper counterpart.
friendlySummary: Grabette records how a person moves objects and turns those actions into data a robot can learn from.
whyItMatters: Researchers and smaller labs can collect diverse manipulation examples without owning a robot for every recording session. Open hardware lowers the entry barrier, although reproducing the system still requires assembly, calibration and careful dataset quality checks.
friendlyWhyItMatters: More people may be able to help train robots without buying a full robot system, though the hardware still needs careful setup.
